        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Speaker diarization failed on PyTorch ≥ 2.6</strong> (<code>Weights only load failed … Unsupported global: torch.torch_version.TorchVersion</code>) even with the pyannote<br>
license accepted. PyTorch 2.6 made <code>torch.load</code> default to<br>
<code>weights_only=True</code>, whose secure unpickler rejects the pyannote checkpoint's<br>
metadata globals. The diarization loader now registers the same safe-globals<br>
allowlist the WhisperX VAD load already uses, so the secure load succeeds.<br>
(<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4576969788" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/270" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/270/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/270" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#270</a>)</li>
</ul>

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Transcription on Windows + NVIDIA failed with <code>Could not locate cudnn_ops_infer64_8.dll</code>.</strong> WhisperX/faster-whisper need cuDNN 8 (via<br>
CTranslate2); when the side-loaded <code>cudnn8_compat</code> libs are missing, the<br>
<strong>PyTorch Whisper</strong> backend (Settings → Models) now works as a drop-in<br>
fallback — it builds its own transformers pipeline on PyTorch's cuDNN-9<br>
stack, with no CTranslate2/cuDNN-8 dependency and no<br>
<code>OMNIVOICE_PRELOAD_TTS_ASR=1</code> required. (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4573685603" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#255</a>)</li>
</ul>

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Settings → About showed the wrong architecture in the Docker/web build.</strong><br>
The "Architecture" row rendered the <em>client browser's</em> platform<br>
(<code>navigator.platform</code> → e.g. "Win32"); it now reports the <strong>server's</strong> CPU<br>
architecture from the backend (<code>platform.machine()</code>), correct for both the<br>
desktop app and Docker. The blank version/GPU/RAM/VRAM in the same report<br>
were the loopback-gate 403s already fixed in v0.3.2. (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4575953079" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/262" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/262/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/262" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#262</a>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>CI</h3>
<ul>
<li>The release SHA-256 checksum step no longer uses <code>mapfile</code> (a bash 4+<br>
builtin) — it broke on the macOS runner's bash 3.2 and dropped the macOS<br>
<code>SHA256SUMS</code> for v0.3.1/v0.3.2. Now portable to bash 3.2.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Linux x64 artifacts</h3>

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>"Loopback origin required" all over the Docker UI</strong> (and a blank version).<br>
The <code>/system/*</code> and <code>/api/settings/*</code> routes are restricted to a loopback<br>
origin, but Docker's NAT makes every request look non-loopback, so the gate<br>
403'd the operator out of the admin UI — including <code>/system/info</code> (blanking<br>
the version) and HF-token entry. The Docker image now runs with<br>
<code>OMNIVOICE_SERVER_MODE=1</code>, which relaxes the gate for the headless<br>
deployment; exposure is governed by the <code>-p</code> port mapping plus the optional<br>
share PIN. Desktop builds are unaffected — their loopback boundary (and the<br>
denial of admin routes to LAN share guests) is unchanged. (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4575938183" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/261" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/261/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/261" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#261</a>)</li>
</ul>

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><p>First tagged build of the 0.3 line off <code>main</code> — it ships the accumulated<br>
<code>[0.3.0]</code> work below plus the fixes here. (The <code>[0.3.0]</code> milestone heading is<br>
kept for the qualitative "actually useful" release.)</p>
<h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Voice-clone / export download crashed in the Docker & browser build</strong> with<br>
<code>TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'invoke')</code>. The<br>
export button called the Tauri save dialog unconditionally; outside the<br>
desktop shell it now falls back to a standard browser download of the file<br>
served at <code>/audio/<path></code>. (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4574611021" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/256" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/256/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/256" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#256</a>)</li>
<li><strong>Docker container showed no version</strong> (a dash) in Settings → About, and the<br>
desktop-only update-channel toggle appeared in the web build. The running<br>
version is now read from the backend (<code>/system/info</code> <code>app_version</code>, <code>/health</code><br>
<code>version</code>); the updater UI is hidden outside Tauri. Also corrected the<br>
version-check command in the Docker docs (<code>omnivoice</code>, not<br>
<code>omnivoice-studio</code>). (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4570688067" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/249" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/249/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/249" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#249</a>)</li>
<li><strong>Transcription failures were masked</strong> by a generic "Transcribe stream<br>
dropped" message. The transcribe SSE stream now surfaces the real, sanitized<br>
cause (with an actionable hint) instead of silently dropping when model load<br>
or VRAM offload fails. (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4573685603" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255/hovercard" href="https://github.com/debpalash/OmniVoice-Studio/issues/255" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#255</a>)</li>
</ul>
<h3>Windows x64 artifacts</h3>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>1f7fb7064f5813330705baa6e0b7fb110fb8257838594e81d51c7935f13ec959 *OmniVoice Studio_0.3.1_x64_en-US.msi
1aa38c6bd0147fadba3a1c083a581ced07ad857eef30ced1e262061c9cf8351a *OmniVoice Studio_0.3.1_x64_en-US.msi.sig
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container position-absolute right-0 top-0">

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Added</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Frameless dictation widget.</strong> Global dictation upgraded from an in-app FAB to a true OS-level floating widget that hovers over any application. Transparent, decorations-free, always-on-top secondary Tauri window activated by <code>⌘+⇧+Space</code>. Auto-hides 2.5 s after a successful paste.</li>
<li><strong>Standalone <code>CaptureWidget</code> component.</strong> Refactored <code>CaptureButton</code> into <code>CaptureWidget</code>, running on an isolated route (<code>/?window=widget</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Social preview image.</strong> Added <code>social-preview.png</code> for GitHub SEO.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Changed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>README overhaul.</strong> Compact 3-column feature grid, reorganized Quickstart (one-command install, Docker, Desktop App tips), updated comparison table, roadmap, and footer CTA.</li>
<li><strong>Docker Compose profiles are mutually exclusive.</strong> CPU service now requires <code>--profile cpu</code> (was the implicit default). Prevents port 3900 conflict when running <code>--profile gpu</code>. Usage: <code>docker compose --profile cpu up</code> or <code>docker compose --profile gpu up</code>.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Fixed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Docker GPU detection false negative.</strong> Preflight reported "No compatible GPU detected" inside Docker containers because <code>nvidia-smi</code> isn't present in the PyTorch base image. The GPU probe now falls back to <code>torch.cuda.is_available()</code> and <code>torch.cuda.get_device_name()</code>, correctly showing CUDA as available in containerized deployments.</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<p>🧪 <strong>Rolling preview build from <code>main</code></strong> — newest features, less tested. Opt in via <strong>Settings → About → Update channel → Preview</strong>; switch back to Stable any time.</p>
</blockquote>

        <div data-pjax="true" data-test-selector="body-content" data-view-component="true" class="markdown-body tmp-my-3"><h3>Added</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Frameless dictation widget.</strong> Global dictation upgraded from an in-app FAB to a true OS-level floating widget that hovers over any application. Transparent, decorations-free, always-on-top secondary Tauri window activated by <code>⌘+⇧+Space</code>. Auto-hides 2.5 s after a successful paste.</li>
<li><strong>Standalone <code>CaptureWidget</code> component.</strong> Refactored <code>CaptureButton</code> into <code>CaptureWidget</code>, running on an isolated route (<code>/?window=widget</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Social preview image.</strong> Added <code>social-preview.png</code> for GitHub SEO.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Changed</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>README overhaul.</strong> Compact 3-column feature grid, reorganized Quickstart (one-command install, Docker, Desktop App tips), updated comparison table, roadmap, and footer CTA.</li>
</ul>
<hr></div>
</div>
